Boeing to Supply 96 Apache Helicopters to Poland
data.description:
By Stephen Nakrosis


Boeing said it will produce 96 AH-64E Apache attack helicopters for the Polish Armed Forces, along with attack helicopters for other international customers, under a foreign military sales contract awarded by the U.S. Army.

The contract is valued at almost $4.7 billion, Boeing added. Deliveries are expected to begin in 2028.

The Polish Ministry of National Defence is already training pilots and maintainers on the attack helicopter.

Boeing said the AH-64E will remain in production until at least 2028, and will serve the U.S. Army and its partner nations as the world's primary attack helicopter into the 2060s.

The deal comes as nations in Europe have begun increasing their spending on defense. Earlier this month, the European Union proposed spending up to $20 billion to improve the transport of weapons and troops, and to support defense technology startups, The Wall Street Journal reported.

In June, members at the North Atlantic Treaty Organization's summit agreed to increase defense spending to 5% of GDP by 2035, according to The Wall Street Journal.


Write to Stephen Nakrosis at stephen.nakrosis@wsj.com


(END) Dow Jones Newswires

11-26-25 1708ET

Hong Kong police say death toll rises to 40 in residential blaze
data.description:
HONG KONG (Reuters) -Hong Kong police said on Thursday the death toll in a fire that ripped through residential blocks in the city had risen to 40, with 45 people still in a critical condition.

A massive fire ripped through multiple high-rise residential blocks in Hong Kong's northern Tai Po district on Wednesday, as authorities struggled to bring the blaze under control.

(Reporting by Joyce Zhou and Edmond Ng, Writing by Anne Marie Roantree; Editing by Nia Williams)

data.title: ICE Canada Weekly Outlook : Slowly Grinding Higher
data.description:
WINNIPEG, Manitoba--ICE canola futures saw some choppy activity during the week ended Nov. 26, falling below nearby support at one point before clawing back from those losses to continue their steady uptrend of the past two months.

Looking at the price action on the chart, canola is "grinding off its October lows," said analyst Mike Jubinville noting that cash basis levels were also improving.

The January contract touched a session low of C$610 per tonne on Oct. 1 but has edged steadily higher since then - settling at C$651.70 per tonne on Nov. 26. Jubinville said a sustained push above C$650 to C$660 would be supportive technically, with the next upside target at C$680.

He added that the market was not overbought at current levels.

However, while canola is trending higher "the fundamental news could change at anytime," said Jubinville. He said weather threats in South America, movement on biodiesel legislation in the United States or any developments with Chinese trade could be the catalysts to spark a larger move one way or the other in the futures.

In the absence of any such news, he expected to see subdued sideways trade through the holiday period.


Source: Commodity News Service Canada, news@marketsfarm.com

(END) Dow Jones Newswires

11-26-25 1704ET

data.title: QBE Announces A$450 Million Buyback, Active Capital Management
data.description:
By Stuart Condie


SYDNEY--Australia's QBE Insurance disclosed plans for a US$291.2 million on-market share buyback and said it will seek to more actively manage capital to optimize returns.

The ASX-listed company on Thursday said it would start buying back shares totaling 450 million Australian dollars in December and continue through 2026. It said the buyback would be funded by surplus capital.

"Based on our medium-term outlook for further mid-single-digit volume growth plus a continuation of strong returns, a more active stance on capital management will be required to ensure group capital, performance and returns are fully optimized," QBE said.

The insurer, which also operates in the U.S., reiterated its 2025 guidance. It also said it expects a 2026 combined operating ratio of about 92.5%, which is in-line with its 2025 outlook.


Write to Stuart Condie at stuart.condie@wsj.com


(END) Dow Jones Newswires

11-26-25 1702ET

Verisure plc Reports Earnings Results for the Third Quarter and Nine Months Ended September 30, 2025
data.description:
Verisure plc reported earnings results for the third quarter and nine months ended September 30, 2025. For the third quarter, the company reported sales was EUR 933 million compared to EUR 854.3 million a year ago. Net loss was EUR 47.8 million compared to EUR 47.2 million a year ago. Basic loss per share from continuing operations was EUR 0.06 compared to EUR 0.06 a year ago. Diluted loss per share from continuing operations was EUR 0.06 compared to EUR 0.06 a year ago.
For the nine months, sales was EUR 2,780.7 million compared to EUR 2,537.8 million a year ago. Net loss was EUR 134.6 million compared to EUR 129.1 million a year ago. Basic loss per share from continuing operations was EUR 0.17 compared to EUR 0.16 a year ago. Diluted loss per share from continuing operations was EUR 0.17 compared to EUR 0.16 a year ago.

Hong Kong resident watches his home burn after leaving dog behind
data.description:
STORY: :: A Hong Kong resident watches his home burn after being forced to leave his dog behind

:: November 26, 2025

:: Jason Kong, Hong Kong resident

"My dog was at home, so I thought of coming back and bringing the dog down from the apartment. It was just around 5 p.m. and the police would not let me up. The fire was not yet burning at our building. I do not know how my dog is doing upstairs now. I am afraid the smoke will suffocate him. // I am devastated. There are so many neighbors and friends. I do not know what is going on anymore. Look, all the apartments are just burning. I do not know what to do."

Kong, who lives in Block One of the Wang Fuk Court housing complex with his wife and dog, said he received a message around 3 p.m. that the roof was on fire and watched in disbelief as the flames spread rapidly.

As he stood among crowds of residents and onlookers staring up at the burning towers, Kong said some of his neighbors were still unaccounted for.

The massive fire, which engulfed multiple blocks of the 32-story Wang Fuk Court complex in Hong Kong's northern Tai Po district, killed at least 36 people and injured more than a dozen others, authorities said. At least 279 people were still unaccounted for, with several dozen hospitalized.

data.title: CBOT wheat futures tick up ahead of US holiday as uncertainty surrounds Ukraine peace talks
Stocks buoyed by rate cut expectations, sterling choppy on UK budget surprise
data.description:
NEW YORK (Reuters) -Global stocks rallied for a fourth straight day on Wednesday as expectations for a U.S. Federal Reserve rate cut remained elevated, while sterling was whipsawed by Britain's fiscal watchdog inadvertently publishing new forecasts before a UK budget release.

On Wall Street, U.S. stocks closed higher, led by gains in the tech sector that rose about 1.5%, in part due to a jump of nearly 7% in Dell Technologies after its quarterly results and outlook. 

Equities have rallied since Friday, when expectations for a December rate cut from the Federal Reserve jumped after New York Fed President John Williams said interest rates can fall in the near term even as other policymakers insisted borrowing costs should remain steady for now. 

Those expectations were buttressed by comments this week from San Francisco Federal Reserve Bank President Mary Daly and Fed Governor Christopher Waller in support of a December cut.

"We've seen some dovish comments from the Fed, particularly the New York Fed ... which I think are signaling a potential cut next month, and I think that that's what's been driving the markets lately," said Matthew Keator, managing partner in the Keator Group, a wealth management firm in Lenox, Massachusetts.

Economic data on Wednesday showed weekly initial jobless claims dropped 6,000 to a seasonally adjusted 216,000 for the week ended November 22, the lowest level since April and below the 225,000 estimate of economists polled by Reuters.

The Dow Jones Industrial Average rose 314.67 points, or 0.67%, to 47,427.12, the S&P 500 gained 46.73 points, or 0.69%, to 6,812.61 and the Nasdaq Composite climbed 189.10 points, or 0.82%, to 23,214.69. 

Expectations for a 25 basis point cut from the Fed held at more than 80%, according to CME's FedWatch Tool, well above the 30.1% from a week ago.

U.S. markets will be closed on Thursday for the Thanksgiving holiday and will have an abbreviated session on Friday.

MSCI's gauge of stocks across the globe jumped 9.31 points, or 0.94%, to 1,000.37, and was on pace for its fourth straight session of gains, its longest streak in a month. The MSCI index has gained 3.3% over the four-day rally, its biggest four-day percentage gain since mid-May.

The pan-European STOXX 600 index closed up 1.09% to record its biggest daily percentage gain in two weeks.

The dollar index, which measures the greenback against a basket of currencies, declined 0.26% to 99.59, with the euro up 0.22% at $1.1594.

Sterling strengthened 0.52% to $1.3234. The currency had swung between a gain of 0.57% and a decline of 0.34% on the day in the wake of the UK budget confusion as the Office for Budget Responsibility's Economic and Fiscal Outlook was released early.

British finance minister Rachel Reeves then announced a big tax-raising budget that will take more money from workers, people saving for a pension and from investors to give herself greater room to meet her deficit-reduction targets.

Ten-year gilt yields were last down 7 basis points at 4.426%.

The Japanese yen weakened 0.25% against the greenback to 156.45 per dollar even as sources told Reuters the Bank of Japan is preparing markets for a possible interest rate hike as soon as next month as it may take a more consistent rate hike path to alter the trajectory of the currency.

The yield on benchmark U.S. 10-year notes shed 1 basis point to 3.992% as the rally in UK government bonds helped limit the downside for longer-dated U.S. debt after stronger-than-expected economic data fueled selling.

The 2-year note yield rose 2 basis points to 3.479%. 

(Additional reporting by Stephen Culp in New York, Johann M Cherian and Pranav Kashyap in Bengaluru, Gregor Stuart Hunter in Singapore, Joice Alves and Marc Jones in London; Editing by Conor Humphries, Nick Zieminski, Will Dunham and Philippa Fletcher)

By Chuck Mikolajczak

data.title: US plans further troop deployments to fight drug trafficking, Hegseth says
data.description:
SANTO DOMINGO (Reuters) -U.S. Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said on Wednesday that the country is aiming to allow further deployments of troops and aircraft to tackle drug trafficking.

Speaking from the Dominican Republic, which has authorized the temporary deployment of U.S. elements, Hegseth said he was hoping other countries would accept U.S. military presence on their soil.

The U.S. has ramped up operations in the Caribbean in recent months, including strikes against alleged drug-trafficking vessels which have killed dozens.

(Reporting by Paul Mathiasen; Writing by Natalia Siniawski; Editing by Kylie Madry)

Canada to Implement New Trade, Financing Measures to Protect Trade-Exposed Sectors -- Update
data.description:
By Paul Vieira

OTTAWA--Canada said Wednesday it would further tighten limits on imported steel and boost financing available to lumber producers as officials try to soften the blow for key parts of the economy battered by President Trump's hefty tariffs.

The additional moves, unveiled by Prime Minister Mark Carney, build on measures previously introduced during the summer, aimed at protecting domestic producers. The new trade measures and financial support reflect the Canadian economy's vulnerability to tariffs of up to 50% on key industrial sectors, and Ottawa's inability to date to clinch a trade deal with Trump that provides relief to exports and reduces elevated trade-policy uncertainty.

"This government will do whatever it takes to protect our sectors, and help them reposition for a new global economy and help them be in a position to build this great country," Carney said at a press conference in the capital. "We need these sectors to build this country."

U.S. and Canadian officials were in talks this fall about a narrower trade deal that would provide tariff relief on steel and aluminum. But Trump suspended talks last month following the broadcast of television commercials in the U.S., paid for by the province of Ontario, that the President said misrepresented comments by former President Ronald Reagan regarding tariffs. No new formal talks are scheduled.

Carney told reporters he will travel to Washington late next week for the World Cup draw to be held at the Kennedy Center, as Canada is a co-host of next year's tournament alongside the U.S. and Mexico. Carney added he spoke to Trump on Tuesday, but didn't elaborate on the conversation other than saying the discussion "was not newsworthy." He expects to see the President at the FIFA event, adding Canada stands ready to restart trade talks when Washington is ready.

The new trade measures cover steel and lumber. Carney said the changes would help strengthen demand from Canadian companies for domestic steel and lumber, and provide a financial bridge for companies and workers as they adapt to a quickly changing global trade environment.

Countries that don't have free-trade agreements with Ottawa will be largely shut out from exporting their steel into Canada without facing a 50% tariff. Levels of steel products from those jurisdictions--among them China, Turkey and Brazil--will be cut to 20% of 2024 levels, from the previous 50% level unveiled in July.

Canada is also limiting imports from countries with free-trade agreements excluding the U.S. and Mexico, to 75% of 2024 levels before a 50% tariff kicks in. U.S. steel has faced a 25% duty since March. There will also be a new 25% global tariff on imported steel-derivative products like wind towers, fasteners and wires, Carney said. About 40% of such products originate in the U.S., although Carney said this levy was not U.S.-focused.

In the past, Carney has bemoaned how much Canada relies on foreign imported steel. About two-thirds of the steel consumed in Canada comes from abroad.

Canada is also boosting the amount in loan guarantees available to forest-products makers, from 700 million Canadian dollars, or the equivalent of about $500 million, to C$1.2 billion. Canada is also pledging to subsidize by up to 50% pan-Canadian freight-rail rates for steel and lumber producers to help ship their wares to customers across the country.

The suite of new measures, "combined with those implemented earlier this year, will allow Canadian steel and lumber producers to benefit from access to the domestic market worth hundreds of millions of dollars," Carney said.

Canada's manufacturing sector has taken a hit from U.S. tariffs. On a year-to-date basis, manufacturing shipments and employment have declined.


Write to Paul Vieira at paul.vieira@wsj.com


(END) Dow Jones Newswires

11-26-25 1621ET

West Virginia receiving 'conflicting reports' about Guard members' condition after shooting, governor says
data.description:
WASHINGTON (Reuters) -West Virginia Governor Patrick Morrisey said he was receiving conflicting reports about the condition of the two National Guards members who were shot on Wednesday in Washington, after earlier saying the two members had died of their injuries.

Morrisey did not elaborate on their condition.

"We are now receiving conflicting reports about the condition of our two Guard members and will provide additional updates once we receive more complete information," Morrisey said in a post on X.

(Reporting by Jasper Ward; editing by Rami Ayyub)

ICE Review : Canola Follows Soy Higher
data.description:
WINNIPEG, Manitoba--The ICE Futures canola market was stronger on Wednesday, with chart-based positioning and spillover from advances in the Chicago soybeans and soyoil providing support.

A move above C$650 per tonne in the January contract was constructive from a chart standpoint, with nearby trendlines pointing higher, according to an analyst.

A lack of significant export demand remained a bearish influence tempering the upside in canola, with a firmer tone in the Canadian dollar also pressuring values.

U.S. markets will be closed for Thanksgiving on Thursday and only open for reduced hours on Friday.

There were 38,815 contracts traded on Wednesday, down from Tuesday when 40,682 contracts changed hands. Spreading accounted for 25,450 of the contracts traded.

Settlement prices in Canadian dollars per metric tonne.

data.title: Blackstone Reportedly Nears $4 Billion Deal for MacLean Power Systems
data.description:
Blackstone Inc. (NYSE:BX) is nearing an acquisition of MacLean Power Systems (MacLean Power, LLC) in a deal that could value the utility-parts maker at more than $4 billion according to people familiar with the matter. The private equity firm may announce a purchase of MacLean from Centerbridge Partners (Centerbridge Partners, L.P.) as soon as next week, the people said, asking not to be identified discussing confidential information. Blackstone beat out other suitors including ABB Ltd. (SWX:ABBN), with the Swiss industrial giant deciding against increasing its offer for MacLean, some of the people said.

While discussions are at an advanced stage, they could still be delayed or falter, the people said. Representatives for Blackstone, Centerbridge and ABB declined to comment. A spokesperson for MacLean couldn?t immediately be reached for comment.
